title: Review: core/src/*.rs security/correctness round 12
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — All new findings are contained in a single module each and have clear, bounded fixes. The two P1 items (FileChunk no cap, auth deadline) affect data received from authenticated peers inside MLS-encrypted sessions and from relay connections respectively. No authentication bypass or privilege escalation found.
Confidence: 85% chance no missed bug in scope. Uncertainty driven by: (1) MLS library (openmls) internals not reviewed — the credential binding issue (nie-kef6.2) may have additional surface not visible here; (2) hpke crate PrivateKey zeroization on drop is crate-internal and not verified; (3) interaction between pow.rs verify_token and relay's replay set not tested end-to-end. Would raise confidence with: access to openmls source to verify credential validation path, and an integration test that verifies auth handshake times out on a misbehaving relay.
